name: Composable Validation Chain
description: Implement a composable validation framework across two modules. Module validator_types.py must define: ValidationError dataclass with fields field (str), message (str), code (str); ValidationResult dataclass with fields valid (bool), errors (list[ValidationError]); Validator abstract base class with abstract method validate(value, field_name: str = '') -> ValidationResult. Module validators.py must import from validator_types.py and implement: Required(); MinLength(n: int); MaxLength(n: int); Range(min_val, max_val) for numbers; Regex(pattern: str); All(*validators) succeeds only if all pass (collects all errors); Any(*validators) succeeds if any pass (returns first failure errors only when all fail); Schema(fields: dict[str, Validator]) validates a dict where each key has its own validator, returning combined errors with proper field paths.
test_imports: from validator_types import ValidationError, ValidationResult, Validator
from validators import Required, MinLength, MaxLength, Range, Regex, All, Any, Schema
signature:
# validator_types.py
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from abc import ABC, abstractmethod

@dataclass
class ValidationError:
    field: str
    message: str
    code: str

@dataclass
class ValidationResult:
    valid: bool
    errors: list = field(default_factory=list)

class Validator(ABC):
    @abstractmethod
    def validate(self, value, field_name: str = '') -> ValidationResult: ...

# validators.py
from validator_types import Validator, ValidationResult, ValidationError

class Required(Validator): ...
class MinLength(Validator): ...
class MaxLength(Validator): ...
class Range(Validator): ...
class Regex(Validator): ...
class All(Validator): ...
class Any(Validator): ...
class Schema(Validator): ...
tests:
def test_range():
    assert Range(0, 10).validate(5).valid is True
    assert Range(0, 10).validate(-1).valid is False
    assert Range(0, 10).validate(11).valid is False

def test_all_combinator():
    v = All(Required(), MinLength(3))
    assert v.validate('abc').valid is True
    assert v.validate('').valid is False
    r = v.validate('ab')
    assert r.valid is False

def test_all_collects_errors():
    v = All(MinLength(5), Regex(r'^\d+$'))
    r = v.validate('ab')
    assert r.valid is False
    assert len(r.errors) == 2

def test_any_combinator():
    v = Any(Range(0, 10), Range(100, 200))
    assert v.validate(5).valid is True
    assert v.validate(150).valid is True
    assert v.validate(50).valid is False

def test_schema_pass():
    s = Schema({'name': Required(), 'age': Range(0, 120)})
    r = s.validate({'name': 'Alice', 'age': 30})
    assert r.valid is True

def test_schema_field_errors():
    s = Schema({'name': Required(), 'age': Range(0, 120)})
    r = s.validate({'name': '', 'age': 200})
    assert r.valid is False
    fields = [e.field for e in r.errors]
    assert 'name' in fields
    assert 'age' in fields

def test_schema_field_path():
    s = Schema({'email': Regex(r'^.+@.+$')})
    r = s.validate({'email': 'invalid'})
    assert r.valid is False
    assert r.errors[0].field == 'email'
constraints: ValidationResult.errors is empty when valid is True
All combinator collects errors from every failing inner validator
Any returns valid=True if at least one inner validator passes
Required treats None and empty string '' as invalid; non-empty values valid
Schema reports field name as the dict key in error.field
ValidationError.code is a short string identifier (e.g. 'required', 'min_length')
